html:
Forward camera translation
Each red arrow points in the direction of camera motion.
⚠ Figure failed to load

elements: Five flat, line-art camera frustum outlines drawn as crisp black wireframes, all aligned along a common horizontal axis (Z-axis)
Thin, sharp black lines outlining frustum sides and bases, with uniform thickness and no 3D shading
Small black dot marking the optical center of each frustum
Sequence of consistently-proportioned thin, flat red arrows pointing right, positioned exactly at each camera center, all parallel to the Z-axis and matching reference schematic style
White background
No shading, gradients, thick edge effects, or lighting—pure 2D technical schematic style

interactions.label: Steps
interactions.teaches: Let's you adjust how many spaced camera positions appear, directly illustrating the motion path sampled over time

interactions.label: Step size
interactions.teaches: Controls the spacing and length of red motion arrows, corresponding to how far the camera moves forward each time

interactions.label: Show full path
interactions.teaches: Switches between seeing all camera positions over time versus only the current position, clarifying how accumulation of steps builds the motion

demo_steps.title: Single step forward
demo_steps.narration: We start by showing just two camera frusta: an initial position and one after a single forward step. The bold red arrow, crisp and thin as in the reference, marks the camera's advance directly along the optical axis — no lateral shift. This is a pure schematic, matching the source image's flat style.

demo_steps.title: Multiple steps show motion sequence
demo_steps.narration: Now, you see several camera positions spaced evenly along the horizontal camera axis. The repeating thin red arrows build a visual rhythm, making it clear how the camera's forward translation accumulates step by step. This illustrates smooth, uninterrupted advance in the schematic's 2D style.

demo_steps.title: Faster camera movement
demo_steps.narration: Increase the step size: the red arrows lengthen and spacing between frusta grows. This sharper layout makes it evident that the camera would cover more distance between each frame, matching the schematic's proportional spacing and emphasizing the key parameter: forward velocity.

demo_steps.title: Focus on current position only
demo_steps.narration: Switch off previous positions so only the final frustum and its arrow remain. This isolates the current state, removing the visual clutter of the motion path and leaving a single, crisp schematic for instantaneous camera location, as in the strictest 2D reference diagrams.

plan.camera_suggestion: Orthographic front view with the Z-axis horizontal from left to right, filling the layout with no perspective, ensuring precise flat schematics with all elements axis-aligned and evenly spaced.
plan.notes: Render all geometry as flat, pure line art. Avoid shading, lighting, or any 3D effects. Use strict 2D line thicknesses and proportions to match a textbook schematic. Arrows must be thin, uniform, and aligned exactly as in the original figure. All camera frusta are identical and evenly spaced along the same axis. If showOldFrusta is off, suppress all but the most recent frustum and arrow. Maintain precise, crisp geometry at all stages.

elements: Five repeated camera frustum shapes oriented with optical axis along a horizontal line
Black circle marking camera center in each frustum
Thin black lines outlining the frustum bases and sides
Bold red horizontal arrows pointing right, starting at the left and aligned with the camera centers
Red arrows passing through or beginning at the black camera center in each frustum
White background

interactions.label: Number of steps
interactions.teaches: Shows how forward motion builds up over time by displaying from a few to many animation steps, illustrating continuous advance

interactions.label: Step size (arrow)
interactions.teaches: Controls the magnitude of each motion step; a larger step size means the camera moves farther along the Z-axis between frames

interactions.label: Show previous locations
interactions.teaches: Toggles whether previous camera positions remain visible, helping relate current position to motion history and visualizing the path of the camera

demo_steps.title: Single forward step
demo_steps.narration: Here, only two camera positions are shown, separated by a single bold red arrow pointing forward. This illustrates the basic setup: the camera moves straight along its optical axis, simulating pure forward motion.

demo_steps.title: Multiple steps: continuous motion
demo_steps.narration: Now, five camera positions are displayed in sequence, each separated by the same distance. The series of red arrows conveys the sense of continuous, steady advance along the Z-axis, as if the camera is moving forward frame by frame.

demo_steps.title: Larger steps: faster motion
demo_steps.narration: Increase the step size: the red arrows grow longer, and the separation between camera positions increases. This shows a faster forward motion—the camera covers more distance between each snapshot.

demo_steps.title: Focus on the current camera
demo_steps.narration: Hide the previous positions so that only the current camera frustum and its incoming arrow are seen. This isolates a single moment, matching how a real camera only knows its current state, not its full trajectory.

interactionPlan.camera_suggestion: Orthographic view from above and slightly to the side, with the Z-axis running horizontally left-to-right, comfortably fitting all frusta and arrows in-frame with margins.
interactionPlan.notes: Keep all frusta identical in orientation and shape, simply translating them along the Z-axis for each step. Use thick bold red for the arrows to emphasize motion direction. If showOldFrusta is false, only draw the last camera and its incoming arrow. If true, render each frustum in order, possibly fading their opacity for older steps.

attempts.html:
Camera forward translation
⚠ Figure failed to load

discrepancies: In the generated figure, the number and positioning of cameras and arrows matches, but the camera frusta are simplified to wireframes with spherical centers rather than filled polygonal shapes as in the 2D source.
Arrow thickness and head shapes are similar but lack the precise 2D style of the source (slightly 3D appearance).
Arrow color and length match, though fade/transparency is used in the generated version to show sequence, while the original has all solid black cameras and arrows.
The original figure aligns everything to a single horizontal (x) axis, but the generated output introduces minor perspective or 3D cues.
No labels are present in the source, and the generated figure is correct in omitting them from the visualization but includes a non-intrusive caption.
evaluation.notes: Faithful recreation with flexible, pedagogical interactivity; minor style differences from 2D, but all elements and core concept are present.
action_items: Adjust camera frustum rendering to be more filled/solid polygonal-like, matching the textbook's bold silhouettes.
Make the arrows flat or more 2D-like (with sharper heads), reducing the 3D tube shine effect.
Reduce perspective foreshortening for a more orthographic, single-plane appearance.

feedback.rationale: The plan is conceptually correct and matches the core elements of the source, but the action items and discrepancies point to generation-level visual and rendering tweaks rather than conceptual restructuring.
action_items: Adjust camera frustum rendering to be more filled/solid polygonal-like, matching the textbook's bold silhouettes.
Make the arrows flat or more 2D-like (with sharper heads), reducing the 3D tube shine effect.
Reduce perspective foreshortening for a more orthographic, single-plane appearance.

Reduce perspective foreshortening for a more orthographic, single-plane appearance.

attempts.html:
Camera forward translation
Red arrows show motion along the camera optical axis.
⚠ Figure failed to load

discrepancies: Interactive sliders, buttons, and a narration caption are present in the generated figure but absent in the source figure
Arrowheads in the generated figure appear visually larger and chunkier compared to the flat arrowheads in the source image
There is a slight gray horizontal band in the center of each frustum in the generated figure not present in the reference
The generated frusta use line thickness and 3D lighting, while the source figure is strictly 2D schematic line art
Arrow alignment, color saturation, and frustum outline style are somewhat different from the flat, diagrammatic look of the source
evaluation.notes: Strong interactive pedagogical value, but the generated figure diverges aesthetically and contains extra elements and 3D effects not in the 2D schematic.
action_items: Remove 3D shading and extra frustum details (e.g., gray band, thick spherical centers) for a flatter 2D look matching the reference schematic.
Modify the arrow designs to match the thinner, flat style and proportions of the source image.
Eliminate the extra UI panel/caption and keep the visual focus strictly on the schematic sequence, as in the reference.
Reduce frustum edge thickness and lighting/shading for pure line drawing aesthetics.
Ensure alignment and overall schematic style strictly match the crispness and proportions of the original.

feedback.rationale: The presence of extra interactive elements, hallucinated features, use of 3D primitives instead of strict 2D schematic lines, and key failure modes like 'Hallucination', 'Wrong-Primitives', and 'Concept-Misunderstood' indicate that the conceptual decomposition needs to be revised to match the schematic nature of the reference.

attempts.html:
Forward camera translation
Each red arrow points in the direction of camera motion.
⚠ Figure failed to load

discrepancies: Interactive controls and UI elements are present in the rendered version but absent in the original textbook schematic
Camera and arrow geometry are slightly heavier and more 3D than the hand-drawn style in the original
There are extra arrows at the end of the sequence in the generated figure, absent in the reference
Spacing between cameras and arrows can be varied and may depart from textbook layout
evaluation.notes: Strong interactivity, accurate camera/arrow arrangement, but some extra arrows and UI differ from the minimal schematic.
action_items: Remove the trailing extra arrow after the last camera to better match the textbook schematic's arrangement.
Optionally, add a toggle to minimize or hide interactive UI for a purer schematic match.
Consider refining the camera outline/arrow thickness for a closer hand-drawn appearance.

feedback.rationale: The issues are related to execution details—removing extra arrows, adjusting outlines, and UI presentation—while the concept and decomposition are correct, so only generation refinement is needed.
